stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies, including transformers and llama.
The focus is on text-generation capabilities within the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama indicates relevance to the competitive landscape of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Polestar has been muscled out of the US market
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Polestar is being barred from selling its electric vehicles in the US market starting in 2027.
The ban is due to a federal government rule restricting vehicles with software originating from China.
This marks a significant market withdrawal for the automotive company in the US.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: OpenAI and Broadcom are collaborating on a specialized chip designed specifically for large language model (LLM) inference.
This development highlights the intensifying 'silicon race' driven by the massive computational demands of modern AI.
The focus on inference at scale addresses a critical bottleneck in deploying LLMs efficiently in real-world applications.

stories.title: Real-Time Voice AI Hears but Does Not Listen
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Leading real-time voice AI systems (OpenAI, Google, Alibaba) fail to interpret vocal delivery cues.
The systems are shown to prioritize spoken words over prosody/vocal tone in complex scenarios.
This failure leads to critical errors, such as mishandling distressed callers or approving risky transactions.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets and optimizes performance on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is running AI functionality entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: IBM claims world’s first sub-1 nanometer chip technology
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: IBM has claimed development of a world-first sub-1 nanometer chip technology.
The technology involves nanostack transistors.
Potential benefits include significant boosts to chip performance or energy efficiency.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of building fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to long-horizon interactions and irreversible actions.
It proposes that Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training inherently contains the necessary components for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for training dedicated, complex reward models in agentic LLM settings.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the vulnerability of current AI safety methods, which rely on controls placed within the agent's own runtime (e.g., prompts, filters).
It introduces the concept of 'escapable AI systems,' where internal runtime controls are susceptible to manipulation by external inputs.
The research proposes a new approach—the 'Unfireable Safety Kernel'—to implement execution-time AI alignment, moving controls outside the agent's immediate address space.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of creating fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to long-horizon and irreversible actions.
Proposes that Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training inherently provides the necessary ingredients for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for the complex and often infeasible training of dedicated reward models in agentic settings.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical gap in MLLM evaluation: the lack of testing for order sensitivity when input order is shuffled.
It introduces 'Facet-Probe,' a comprehensive five-facet audit designed to rigorously test the reliability of frontier and open-weight MLLMs.
The methodology uses a Bayesian item-response model to effectively separate inherent ordering noise from model-specific biases.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the security and safety of AI agents that interact with external tools and APIs.
It critiques current safety methods (like system prompts and output filters) as insufficient because they reside within the agent's own runtime, making them vulnerable.
The authors introduce the concept of 'escapable AI systems' and propose new authorization mechanisms to ensure safety outside the agent's direct control.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: Investigates the catastrophic collapse observed in Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLMs.
Identifies that instability in tool-use tasks often stems from unexpected probability spikes in control tokens.
Proposes that incorporating supervisory signals can effectively resolve the performance degradation issues in agentic RL.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in MLLM evaluation: current benchmarks fail to test for order sensitivity in multimodal inputs.
Introduces 'Facet-Probe,' a comprehensive five-facet audit designed to rigorously test MLLMs across various ordering scenarios.
Uses a Bayesian item-response model to effectively distinguish between inherent model bias and noise introduced by input ordering.

stories.title: Detect, Unlearn, Restore: Defending Text Summarization Models Against Data Poisoning
stories.author: Shirin Nilizadeh
bullet_points: Addresses the critical security threat of data poisoning during the fine-tuning of LLMs for text summarization.
Highlights that poisoning can induce persistent, subtle failures (e.g., biased summaries) while bypassing standard evaluation metrics.
Proposes a unified post-hoc defense framework for detecting and remedying these adversarial attacks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Feds deny Polestar authorization to sell cars in US from model year 2027
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Federal authorities have denied Polestar authorization to sell vehicles in the United States.
This denial applies to Polestar models starting from the 2027 model year.
The situation differs from Volvo, which has received authorization to sell in the US.

stories.title: New effort will get genome sequences for entire Endangered Species list
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Colossal Biosciences is undertaking a project to sequence genomes for all endangered species.
The initiative involves biobanking tissues from these endangered species.
This represents a significant application of advanced genomics and biobanking technology.

stories.title: Learning Action Priors for Cross-embodiment Robot Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which often lack explicit motion priors.
Existing VLA models rely on VLM backbones but force the action module to learn physical motion largely from scratch.
The research proposes learning action priors to simplify the learning process, avoiding the simultaneous discovery of temporal dynamics and cross-modal alignment.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using a single model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach can reduce output diversity and flatten pass@k curves.
The researchers attribute this reduction in diversity to compounding biases inherent in using sampled demonstrations for self-distillation.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Model Forensics' as a new approach to AI safety, moving beyond simply detecting concerning model behavior.
It addresses the limitation that concerning behavior alone doesn't prove misalignment, as actions can stem from benign causes like confusion.
A baseline two-step protocol is proposed for model forensics to investigate if concerning actions are driven by malign intent.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously unlearns previously acquired rules during continued pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal (e.g., losing pronoun-gender agreement) can occur without any discernible change in the model's loss curve.
This suggests that the training corpus itself dictates which learned knowledge persists, offering new insights into catastrophic forgetting and knowledge retention in LLMs.

stories.title: TriViewBench: Controlled Complexity Scaling for Multi-View Structural Reasoning in MLLMs
stories.author: Lan-Zhe Guo
bullet_points: Introduces TriViewBench, a new benchmark designed to test Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) on structural reasoning.
The benchmark uses synthetic 3D scenes with controlled parameters like object count and occlusion to measure scalability.
It organizes 1,923 scenes into four distinct complexity levels to rigorously test model robustness beyond standard QA benchmarks.

stories.title: Can Trustless Agents Be Trusted? An Empirical Study of the ERC-8004 Decentralized AI Agent Ecosystem
stories.author: Zhipeng Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical trust challenge in decentralized AI agent ecosystems as agents interact across organizational boundaries.
Introduces ERC-8004, a permissionless trust layer utilizing on-chain registries for Identity, Reputation, and Validation.
The study aims to empirically validate the effectiveness of this protocol despite its rapid adoption.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using one model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach can reduce output diversity and flatten pass@k curves.
The authors attribute this reduction in diversity to compounding biases inherent in using sampled demonstrations for self-distillation.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Model Forensics' as a new approach to AI safety research.
It addresses the limitation of current methods, noting that concerning behavior alone does not prove model misalignment.
The proposed protocol involves investigating the underlying cause of an action to determine if it stems from malign intent rather than benign confusion.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
Demonstrates this reversal by showing a model initially learning a pronoun-gender rule (0.94 accuracy) and later forgetting it (near zero accuracy) without a corresponding loss curve change.
Suggests that the training corpus itself dictates which learned rules a model retains, offering a new insight into model stability and knowledge retention.

stories.title: FedReLa: Imbalanced Federated Learning via Re-Labeling
stories.author: Liuhua Peng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of class imbalance in Federated Learning (FL), a critical issue in decentralized training.
Proposes 'FedReLa,' a method utilizing re-labeling to mitigate performance degradation caused by data heterogeneity.
Highlights that the mismatch between local and global class distributions significantly hinders standard FL model aggregation.

stories.title: When Certainty Is an Artifact: Keyword Lexicon Blindness and the (Mis)Measurement of Rhetorical Stance
stories.author: Bo Chen
bullet_points: The paper investigates whether statistically significant findings in computational social science can be measurement artifacts.
It demonstrates that a strong correlation between negative affect and 'emphatic certainty' was found using keyword-based scoring.
Replacing keyword counting with LLM-based semantic classification significantly alters the measurement of rhetorical stance.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal without any corresponding change in the model's loss curve, suggesting an internal, non-gradient-based mechanism.
The research highlights the unpredictable and dynamic nature of knowledge retention within large language models.

stories.title: How Robust is OCR-Reasoning? Evaluating OCR-Reasoning Robustness of Vision-Language Models under Visual Perturbations
stories.author: Yi Chang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of understanding regarding the robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) when performing OCR-based reasoning.
It highlights that visual perturbations (like degradation) can cause OCR errors and structural distortions, impacting reasoning accuracy.
The authors introduce 'OCR-Robust,' a new benchmark specifically designed to systematically evaluate this robustness issue.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The paper investigates instability issues, specifically catastrophic collapse, in Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods for LLM tool-use.
The research identifies that performance degradation in tool-use tasks often stems from unexpected probability spikes in control tokens.
The authors propose that incorporating supervisory signals can effectively resolve these RL-based tool-use failures and improve model stability.

stories.title: The Tatoxa System for Text Detoxification in Low-Resource Languages: The Case of Tatar
stories.author: Alexander Panchenko
bullet_points: Introduces 'Tatoxa,' a novel system designed for text detoxification (harmful content detection/mitigation).
Addresses a critical gap in NLP research by focusing on low-resource languages, specifically Tatar.
Demonstrates that the proposed system outperforms existing open-source and proprietary commercial LLMs in its task.

stories.title: Dziri Voicebot: An End-to-End Low-Resource Speech-to-Speech Conversational System for Algerian Dialect
stories.author: Asma Kemmoum
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of bias in AI/ML models towards high-resource languages.
Focuses on developing an end-to-end speech-to-speech system specifically for Algerian Dialect.
Tackles significant linguistic challenges, including non-standard orthography and code-switching with French.

stories.title: IBM debuts sub-1 nanometer chip technology
stories.author: porridgeraisin
bullet_points: IBM has announced the debut of new chip technology.
The technology operates at a sub-nanometer scale.
The announcement originates from Hacker News, suggesting industry interest.

stories.title: Lite-Harness SDK
stories.author: jeancarlosn
bullet_points: Introduces a 'Lite-Harness SDK' designed to unify AI coding interfaces.
Allows developers to seamlessly swap between different AI coding harnesses (e.g., Claude Code, Codex).
Addresses the need for standardized APIs when integrating various AI coding assistants into development workflows.

stories.title: The agent-first approach to building products
stories.author: adamklein
bullet_points: The article discusses the 'agent-first' paradigm in product development.
It posits that future platform relevance will depend on agent compatibility.
The content suggests a significant shift towards AI agents driving customer interaction by 2026.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for AI agent competition.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition improves AI reasoning and training data.
Focuses on the potential for competitive environments to drive better model development.
